What would the entry look like if a HTF FVG is split between the premium and discount zones? Just enter once price retraces deep enough to touch the desired zone?

The key with FVGs is waiting for that clean mitigation before entry. I teach my students to look for multiple timeframe confluence when these gaps form at premium or discount levels. The rejection candle structure after the gap fills is usually what separates the high probability setups from the noise.

This is a masterclass in keeping scalping clean. Overloading charts is a trap; the real magic happens when you respect the first 5-minute range and pre-market levels. Using that 'One Candle Rule' to track order flow at the NY open is exactly how you identify who is actually in control of the price. If you’re tired of bouncing between strategies, stick to this one clear model. Discipline over noise, every single time.

For anyone new to crypto: stop looking at the 1-minute charts. I lost so much money early on because I was reacting to every tiny wiggle. Once I started looking at the higher timeframes and understanding institutional flow, my stress levels dropped and my hits started landing. Trading is 90% patience and 10% execution..

This actually helped me understand why not every FVG is worth trading, especially the whole premium vs discount part. I’ve been trying to focus more on structure and liquidity sweeps first, then letting the first light beacon help me map the higher timeframe zones cleaner before dropping down for entries. The FVG inside FVG idea makes a lot more sense when you treat the higher one as the reaction area and the lower one as timing. Definitely a good reminder to wait for confirmation instead of just setting blind limits.
